name: pyprind

metadata.name: PyPrind
metadata.summary: Python Progress Bar and Percent Indicator Utility

documentation:
The PyPrind (Python Progress Indicator) module provides a progress
bar and a percentage indicator
object that let you track the progress of a loop structure or
other iterative computation.
Typical applications include the processing of
large data sets to provide an intuitive estimate
at runtime about the progress of the computation.

For more details and examples please see the package documentation
https://github.com/rasbt/pyprind/blob/master/README.md.

Contact
=============

If you have any questions or comments about PyPrind,
please feel free to contact me via
email: mail@sebastianraschka.com
or Twitter: https://twitter.com/rasbt

classes.name: pyprind.progpercent.ProgPercent

methods.name: pyprind.progpercent.ProgPercent._print
documentation: Prints formatted percentage and tracked time to the screen.

Initializes a progress bar object that allows visuzalization
of an iterational computation in the standard output screen.

Parameters
----------
iterations : `int`
    Number of iterations for the iterative computation.
track_time : `bool` (default: `True`)
    Prints elapsed time when loop has finished.
stream : `int` (default: 2).
    Setting the output stream.
    Takes `1` for stdout, `2` for stderr, or a custom stream object
title : `str` (default: `''`).
    Setting a title for the percentage indicator.
monitor : `bool` (default: `False`)
    Monitors CPU and memory usage if `True` (requires `psutil` package).
update_interval : float or int (default: `None`)
    The update_interval in seconds controls how often the progress
    is flushed to the screen.
    Automatic mode if `update_interval=None`.

classes.name: pyprind.progbar.ProgBar

methods.name: pyprind.progbar.ProgBar._adjust_width
documentation: Shrinks bar if number of iterations is less than the bar width

Initializes a progress bar object that allows visuzalization
of an iterational computation in the standard output screen.

Parameters
----------
iterations : `int`
    Number of iterations for the iterative computation.
track_time : `bool` (default: `True`)
    Prints elapsed time when loop has finished.
width : `int` (default: 30)
    Sets the progress bar width in characters.
stream : `int` (default: 2).
    Setting the output stream.
    Takes `1` for stdout, `2` for stderr, or a custom stream object
title : `str` (default:  `''`)
    Setting a title for the progress bar.
monitor : `bool` (default: False)
    Monitors CPU and memory usage if `True` (requires `psutil` package).
update_interval : float or int (default: None)
    The update_interval in seconds controls how often the progress
    is flushed to the screen.
    Automatic mode if update_interval=None.

methods.name: pyprind.prog_class.Prog.__init__
documentation: Initializes tracking object.

methods.name: pyprind.prog_class.Prog.update
documentation:
Updates the progress bar / percentage indicator.

Parameters
----------
iterations : int (default: 1)
    default argument can be changed to integer values
    >=1 in order to update the progress indicators more than once
    per iteration.
item_id : str (default: None)
    Print an item_id sring behind the progress bar
force_flush : bool (default: False)
    If True, flushes the progress indicator to the output screen
    in each iteration.

methods.name: pyprind.prog_class.Prog.stop
documentation: Stops the progress bar / percentage indicator if necessary.

methods.name: pyprind.prog_class.Prog._check_stream
documentation: Determines which output stream (stdout, stderr, or custom) to use

methods.name: pyprind.prog_class.Prog._elapsed
documentation: Returns elapsed time at update.

methods.name: pyprind.prog_class.Prog._calc_eta
documentation: Calculates estimated time left until completion.

methods.name: pyprind.prog_class.Prog._calc_percent
documentation: Calculates the rel. progress in percent with 2 decimal points.

methods.name: pyprind.prog_class.Prog._no_stream
documentation: Called when no valid output stream is available.

methods.name: pyprind.prog_class.Prog._finish
documentation: Determines if maximum number of iterations (seed) is reached.

methods.name: pyprind.prog_class.Prog._print_title
documentation: Prints tracking title at initialization.

methods.name: pyprind.prog_class.Prog._cache_eta
documentation: Prints the estimated time left.

methods.name: pyprind.prog_class.Prog._cache_item_id
documentation: Prints an item id behind the tracking object.
